# Software Requirements Specification
## Inventory Management System

### Document Information
- **Document Title:** Software Requirements Specification for Inventory Management System
- **Version:** 1.0
- **Date:** March 10, 2023
- **Domain:** Construction
- **Status:** Final

---

4. External Interface Requirements

## 1. Introduction

### 1.1 Purpose
This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document outlines the requirements for an Inventory Management System designed to manage inventory levels, track stock movements, and provide real-time notifications.

### 1.2 Scope
The system will be developed using a combination of web technologies (HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript), backend frameworks (Node.js, Express.js), and databases (MongoDB). The scope includes the development of the following features:

* User authentication and authorization
* Inventory management (add, update, delete items)
* Stock tracking (track stock movements, low stock alerts)
* Reporting (generate comprehensive reports on inventory usage)

### 1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, and Abbreviations

| Term | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| SRS | Software Requirements Specification |
| HTML5 | HyperText Markup Language version 5 |
| CSS3 | Cascading Style Sheets version 3 |
| JavaScript | Programming language for client-side scripting |
| Node.js | Runtime environment for executing JavaScript code on the server |
| Express.js | Framework for building web applications using Node.js |
| MongoDB | NoSQL database management system |

### 1.4 References

* [Inventory Management System Requirements](https://example.com/inventory-management-system-requirements)
* [Construction Industry Standards](https://example.com/construction-industry-standards)

### 1.5 Overview
This SRS document is organized into the following sections:

* Overall Description (Section 2): Provides an overview of the system, its functions, and operating environment.
* System Features (Section 3): Describes the functional requirements of the system, including user authentication, inventory management, stock tracking, and reporting.
* External Interface Requirements (Section 4): Outlines the interface requirements for users, hardware, software, and communication interfaces.
* Non-Functional Requirements (Section 5): Specifies the performance, security, reliability, usability, maintainability, and portability requirements of the system.
* System Models (Section 6): Describes the data model, process model, and system architecture of the system.
* Verification and Validation (Section 7): Outlines the methods for verifying and validating the system's requirements.

## 2. Overall Description

### 2.1 Product Perspective
The Inventory Management System is designed to manage inventory levels, track stock movements, and provide real-time notifications for construction companies.

### 2.2 Product Functions
The system will perform the following functions:

* User authentication and authorization
* Inventory management (add, update, delete items)
* Stock tracking (track stock movements, low stock alerts)
* Reporting (generate comprehensive reports on inventory usage)

### 2.3 User Classes and Characteristics

| User Class | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Administrator | Responsible for managing user accounts, inventory levels, and reporting |
| Inventory Manager | Responsible for adding, updating, and deleting items in the inventory |
| Warehouse Staff | Responsible for tracking stock movements and low stock alerts |

### 2.4 Operating Environment
The system will be deployed on a cloud-based infrastructure with the following specifications:

* Hardware: Cloud-based servers (AWS, Google Cloud)
* Software: Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB
* Database: MongoDB

### 2.5 Design and Implementation Constraints
The system must comply with the following constraints:

* Security: Implement robust security measures to protect user data and inventory levels.
* Performance: Ensure the system can handle high traffic and large datasets.
* Scalability: Design the system to scale horizontally and vertically.

### 2.6 Assumptions and Dependencies

| Assumption | Description |
| --- | --- |
| User authentication | Users will be authenticated using a secure login mechanism. |
| Inventory data | Inventory levels will be updated in real-time. |

## 3. System Features

### 3.1 Functional Requirements
The following functional requirements are extracted from the user stories:

* **FR-001**: The system should allow users to log in and authenticate their identity.
	+ Priority: High
	+ Source: US-001
	+ Acceptance Criteria:
		- Users can log in using a secure login mechanism.
		- Users can access their account information after logging in.
* **FR-002**: The system should allow users to add, update, and delete items in the inventory.
	+ Priority: Medium
	+ Source: US-002
	+ Acceptance Criteria:
		- Users can add new items to the inventory.
		- Users can update existing items in the inventory.
		- Users can delete items from the inventory.

### 3.2 Business Rules

| Business Rule | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Inventory levels must be updated in real-time | The system will automatically update inventory levels when a user adds, updates, or deletes an item. |

## 4. External Interface Requirements

### 4.1 User Interfaces
The system will have the following user interfaces:

* Web interface: Users can access the system using a web browser.
* Mobile app: Users can access the system using a mobile app.

### 4.2 Hardware Interfaces
The system will interact with the following hardware devices:

* Barcode scanners: Used to scan barcodes and update inventory levels.
* Printers: Used to print reports and labels.

### 4.3 Software Interfaces
The system will integrate with the following software applications:

* Accounting software: Used to track financial transactions and inventory levels.
* CRM software: Used to manage customer relationships and sales data.

### 4.4 Communications Interfaces
The system will communicate with the following external systems:

* APIs: Used to retrieve and update inventory levels in real-time.
* Web services: Used to integrate with other applications and services.

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

### 5.1 Performance Requirements
The system must meet the following performance requirements:

* Response time: The system should respond within 2 seconds for all user interactions.
* Throughput: The system should handle up to 100 concurrent users.

### 5.2 Security Requirements
The system must meet the following security requirements:

* Authentication: Users must be authenticated using a secure login mechanism.
* Authorization: Users must have access control based on their roles and permissions.

### 5.3 Reliability Requirements
The system must meet the following reliability requirements:

* Availability: The system should be available 99.9% of the time.
* Fault tolerance: The system should be able to recover from failures and errors.

### 5.4 Usability Requirements
The system must meet the following usability requirements:

* User experience: The system should provide a user-friendly interface for users to interact with.
* Accessibility: The system should be accessible on various devices and browsers.

### 5.5 Maintainability Requirements
The system must meet the following maintainability requirements:

* Code quality: The code should follow best practices and be easy to understand.
* Documentation: The system should have comprehensive documentation for maintenance and support.

### 5.6 Portability Requirements
The system must meet the following portability requirements:

* Platform independence: The system should run on various platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux).
* Database independence: The system should use a database that is independent of the platform.

## 6. System Models

### 6.1 Data Model
The data model for the system includes the following entities and relationships:

| Entity | Description |
| --- | --- |
| User | Represents a user with attributes (username, password, role). |
| Item | Represents an item in the inventory with attributes (name, description, quantity). |
| Inventory | Represents the inventory levels of items with attributes (item ID, quantity). |

### 6.2 Process Model
The process model for the system includes the following processes and workflows:

* User authentication: Users log in to access their account information.
* Item management: Users add, update, or delete items in the inventory.
* Reporting: The system generates reports on inventory usage.

### 6.3 System Architecture
The system architecture includes the following components:

* Frontend: Web interface and mobile app for user interaction.
* Backend: Node.js and Express.js for server-side logic and API integration.
* Database: MongoDB for storing data.

## 7. Verification and Validation

### 7.1 Verification Methods
The following verification methods will be used to ensure the system meets its requirements:

* Unit testing: Test individual components of the system.
* Integration testing: Test how components interact with each other.
* System testing: Test the entire system for functionality and performance.

### 7.2 Validation Criteria
The following validation criteria will be used to ensure the system meets user needs:

* User acceptance testing (UAT): Users test the system for usability and functionality.
* Business acceptance testing (BAT): Business stakeholders test the system for business requirements.

### 7.3 Testing Requirements
The following testing requirements must be met:

* Test cases: Develop comprehensive test cases to cover all scenarios.
* Test data: Use realistic test data to simulate real-world scenarios.
* Test environment: Set up a test environment that mirrors production.
